text:
Admission Date:  1971-09-11              Discharge Date:   2007-10-04


Service: NEUROLOGY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Lawrence
Chief Complaint:
unresponiveness

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
-R-hemicraniotomy

History of Present Illness:
Given pt's mental status, she was unable to give a history, so
history from pt's nephew and medical record.
.
HPI: 83 year-old woman with a history of afib on coumadin, HTN,
CAD, high cholesterol, DM presents with left hemiplegia, right
gaze deviation, and decreased responsiveness for an unknown
amount of time.
.
Nephew reports that he last saw pt on 02-08 and she was doing
well. Home health aide saw pt on 12-03, and pt also doing well.
Nephew says that he called her on Sunday 08-13 as he usually does
since he usually lays out allof her pills for the week. She did
not answer, and he left a message, which he reports is not that
unusual. He called her again last night as he had not heard from
her, and left another message. He then received phone call today
from Home Health agency that the aide was at apartment and could
hear noises inside but pt did not answer door. Nephew called 911
and went to her house.
.
Per EMS report, pt found lying on her back in bed. She was
minimally resposnive, only responding to pain with minimal
right-
sided movement. She did not move her left side. Breathing was
shallow, radial pulse was irregular and weak, capillary refill
was poor, skin was hot and diaphoretic. Vitals in the field were
230/100, HR 88-92, RR 24-29, FSBS 209.
.
On arrival in Kelsey Macias, pt remained minimally responsive with left-
sided plegia. She was therefore intubated for airway protection.
She was tachycardic to the 170s with unclear rhythm, possibly
aflutter vs SVT, with BP 160s. She was given 6mg adenosine to
slow HR and try to identify rhythm. She then dropped BP to 60s
systolic despite IVF and was cardioverted x2 at 200J. HR slowed
to 100s with sinus rhythm and PACs and SBP 100s.
.
ROS: Unable given mental status/intubation


Past Medical History:
1. Diabetes, type 2
2. Afib on coumadin, on amio in past
3. h/o aflutter, s/p DCCV
4. Pulmonary HTN
5. HTN
6. CAD s/p NSTEMI 1974
7. CHF/cardiomyopathy 07-15 fen-phen, stopped drug 1974. Most
recent echo with EF 55-60%, 2+ MR, 2+ TR, mild LVH
8. Obesity
9. Hypercholesterolemia
10. Sleep-disordered breathing


Social History:
Lives alone. She denies use of alcohol, tobacco, or illicit
drugs.
Her nephew Mr. Joshua Cook Kelsey Macias is her health care proxy.

Family History:
++Stroke--sister age 69-70, nephew age 34


Physical Exam:
T 100.5  BP 230/110-->60s/20s-->100s/40s  HR 170s-->100s
RR 20-24, shallow  O2 sat 100% NRB
General: Appears stated age, minimally responsive
HEENT: NC/AT Sclera anicteric. Dry MM
Neck: Supple.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation anterolaterally
CV: Irreg, nl S1, S2, no murmur. 2+ carotids without bruit
Abd: Soft, nontender, normoactive bowel sounds
Extr: Mild edema, poor cap refill, weak radial pulses
.
Neurologic Examination: (pre-intubation)
Mental Status: Opens eyes to loud voice/stimulation. Right gaze
deviation. Shows thumb to command, moves toes to command.
Minimal
verbal output. Drifts back to sleep without continued
stimulation.
.
Cranial Nerves: Did not blink to threat, bilaterally. Pupils
equally round and reactive to light, 3 to 2 mm bilaterally.
Right
gaze deviation, but does cross midline on doll's. Severe left
facial droop. Decreased corneal on left.
.
Motor: Normal tone, bulk bilaterally. No fasciculations or
tremor. Moves right side spontaneously and to command, at least
distally. Left arm and leg plegic with no spontaneous movement
or
to command, and only very minimal foot withdrawal to noxious.
.
Sensation: Withdraws/localizes noxious on right. Grimaces and
increased right-sided movmements to noxious on left though no
motor response.
.
Reflexes: DTRs decreased but symmetric at biceps, absent at BR,
patellar, ankle. Toes were mute bilaterally
.
Unable to assess coordination and gait given mental status.


Pertinent Results:
1971-09-11 03:22PM   WBC-15.2*# RBC-4.74 HGB-13.9# HCT-44.7 MCV-94
MCH-29.3 MCHC-31.0 RDW-15.1
1971-09-11 03:22PM   NEUTS-70.9* LYMPHS-22.7 MONOS-5.8 EOS-0.3
BASOS-0.4
1971-09-11 03:22PM   PLT COUNT-221
1971-09-11 03:22PM   PT-16.0* PTT-21.5* INR(PT)-1.7
1971-09-11 03:22PM   TRIGLYCER-157* HDL CHOL-83 CHOL/HDL-3.1
LDL(CALC)-144*
1971-09-11 03:22PM   TOT PROT-7.3 ALBUMIN-4.0 GLOBULIN-3.3
CALCIUM-9.6 PHOSPHATE-4.1 MAGNESIUM-2.4 CHOLEST-258*
1971-09-11 03:22PM   ALT(SGPT)-18 AST(SGOT)-42* CK(CPK)-261* ALK
PHOS-61 AMYLASE-66 TOT BILI-1.1
1971-09-11 03:22PM   GLUCOSE-169* UREA N-42* CREAT-1.5*
SODIUM-153* POTASSIUM-4.0 CHLORIDE-110* TOTAL CO2-28 ANION
GAP-19
1971-09-11 05:18PM   %HbA1c-6.9* [Hgb]-DONE [A1c]-DONE
.
CT head without contrast 04-20:
There are multiple large right-sided right frontal hematomas.
Adjacent areas of low attenuation extend to and involve the
cortex. The distribution is in the anterior cerebral artery and
anterior division of the middle cerebral artery territory. These
findings could be consistent with a hemorrhagic stroke given the
appearance and distribution. There is 5 mm worth of leftward
midline shift. The perimesencephalic cisterns are preserved.
There are a few small areas of subarachnoid blood within the
left frontal region.
Incidental note is made of a large left maxillary retention cyst
or polyp.
IMPRESSION: Multiple large hematomas within the right frontal
lobe could be consistent with a hemorrhagic infarct but vascular
malformation and neoplasm are not excluded. Minimal midline
shift. Perimesencephalic cisterns are intact.
.
Carotid series:
Unremarkable exam. No stenosis in the extracranial carotid
arteries.
.
TTE:
The left atrium is elongated. The right atrium is moderately
dilated. No
atrial septal defect or patent foramen ovale is seen by 2D,
color Doppler or  saline contrast with maneuvers. There is mild
symmetric left ventricular  hypertrophy with normal cavity size
and systolic function (LVEF>55%). No  masses or thrombi are seen
in the left ventricle. Right ventricular chamber  size and free
wall motion are normal. The aortic valve leaflets (3) are mildly
 thickened but aortic stenosis is not present. No aortic
regurgitation is seen.
The mitral valve leaflets are mildly thickened. There is no
mitral valve
prolapse. Mild to moderate (01-20+) mitral regurgitation is seen.
The tricuspid  valve leaflets are mildly thickened. The
estimated pulmonary artery systolic  pressure is normal. There
is a small pericardial effusion. There are no  echocardiographic
signs of tamponade.
.
CT head without contrast 08-15: There is a large area of low
attenuation in the right frontal, parietal, and temporal lobes,
consistent with an infarction, which demonstrates slightly
decreased mass effect compared to 03-27, consistent with
evolution of infarction. Previously noted multifocal blood
products within this infarction appear less dense than
previously. There is no evidence of acute rebleeding. The soft
tissues overlying the right craniectomy site are not
significantly changed. The frontal Clark of the right lateral
ventricle is less compressed. Periventricular white matter
hypodensity in the left cerebral hemisphere appears unchanged.
Decreased mass effect associated with the large right middle and
anterior cerebral territory infarction. Evolution of blood
products within the infarction without evidence of
rebleeding.Compared with the findings of the prior report (tape
unavailable for review)  of 1998-01-09, significant pulmonary
hypertension is no longer detected.  Otherwise, probably no
change.


Brief Hospital Course:
The patient is an 83 yo woman with a h/o DM, HTN, chol, CAD,
CHF, afib on coumadin, who was found in her apt on bed, with
left hemiplegia and right gaze deviation. As she was minimally
responsive whe was intubated. A head CT showed a right
ACA/superior division MCA stroke with hemorrhagic
transformation. CXR suggested LLL atelectasis vs consolidation.
.
Neuro:
Exam upon dishcarge: the patient can be woken up. She has an eye
opening aparaxia, which makes it impossible for her to open her
eyes to command. She is able to wiggle her R-toes and squeeze
her R-hand/show her R thumb when asked. She has hemiplegia in
her L-arm and leg, with only minimal movement in her L-leg to
noxious stimuli and no movement in her L-arm. The patient is
non-verbal.
The patient underwent a R-hemicraniotomy 11-01 as a 1cm midline
shift was noted shortly after presentation. The skull is
currently preserved in her abdominal wall. Repositing of the
skull by neurosurgery should be planned for in 05-09 wks after the
craniotomy (see follow up appointment). To protect her brain,
she should wear a helmet. In addition, she is on seizure
prophylaxis (dilantin; goal 15-20; please follow level regularly
taking into account her albumin level). She will need to be on
prophylaxis for a few years.
An MRI/MRA should be scheduled in 05-09 weeks to look for a
possible underlying cause of the stroke. Repeat CT's of the head
 (10-08 and (/8) showed no interval change apart from progressive
reduction of the edema. CTA of carotids showed no stenosis and a
TTE did not show a thromboembolic source or wall motion
abnormalities.
.
Respiratory:
The patient remained dependent on ventilator support. She
tolerated CPAP well, but has episode of apnea (30sec) that might
be central of origin. She was trached on 11-09. Please continue
tracheotomy care.
.
Cardiovascular:
The patient has a history of Afibb. She was in NSR during most
of the hospitalization but returned to 342 Monica Viaduct Suite 965
Alvarezchester, MS 17799 on 10-05. At this
point the metoprolol was increased. Amiodarone was not give as
her rate remained well controled. Coumadin should be kept on
hold given the large intracranial hemorrhages. Aspirin may be
restarted for secondary stroke prophylaxix in 4 weeks.
.
Endocrine:
The patient has DM. She was maintained on a ISS and NPH 18 units
in AM and 18 units PM. Her FSBS were reasonably controlled.
Further adjustments to the NPH should be made depending on her
FSBS.
.
Infectious disease:
The patient received cefazolin peri-operative. She was started
on levaquin for a LLL PNA. She developed a fever on 08-15 after
which her CVL was discontinued (bacteremia). Final results of
the cultures are pending. A PICC line was inserted 06-04 and
other lines d/c-ed at that point. Levaquin to be continued for 4
more days.
.
Haematology:
The patient's Hct dropped to 26.6 on 08-15. There were no signs of
GI bleed and this drop might have been related to the bacteremia
that was noted that day. Her Hct has remained stable otherwise
around 30. She received GI prophylaxis.
.
Renal:
Upon admission her creatinin and sodium were markedly elevated
secondary to dehydration. Creatinin and sodium normalized slowly
after sufficient rehydration. The patient needs diuresis PRN,
based on her daily weigth.
.
Prophylaxis:
Please continue penumo boots, PPI, heparin sc TID and a bowel
regimen (including senna, dulcolax and colace).
.
FEN:
Tube feeds per PEG tube at goal rate. PEG placed 11-09. PICC
placed 06-04.
.
Code: full;
HCP is nephew Mr. Joshua Cook Michele Williams cell 760-652-1475, home
[**0-0-**]


Medications on Admission:
Coumadin 3, ambien 10, hydralazine 50 q6h, cozaar 100,
lasix 40, metoprolol 25 tid, metformin 500 Hemet Valley Health Care Center, lipitor 20, ASA
81, MVI, calcium, colace, senna


Discharge Medications:
1. Heparin (Porcine) 5,000 unit/mL Solution Sig: One (1) 5000
units Injection TID (3 times a day): until patient mobilized.
2. Atorvastatin 20 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
3. Nystatin 100,000 unit/mL Suspension Sig: 5-10 MLs PO QID (4
times a day) as needed.
4. Miconazole Nitrate 2 % Powder Sig: One (1) Appl Topical  TID
(3 times a day) as needed.
5. Senna 8.6 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO BID (2 times a
day) as needed.
6. Docusate Sodium 100 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2
times a day).
7. Bisacodyl 5 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: Two (2)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily) as needed.
8. Acetaminophen 160 mg/5 mL Solution Sig: One (1) 650 PO Q4-6H
(every 4 to 6 hours) as needed for fever.
9. Polyvinyl Alcohol 1.4 % Drops Sig: 1-2 Drops Ophthalmic  PRN
(as needed).
10. Phenytoin 50 mg Tablet, Chewable Sig: Two (2) 100mg PO TID
(3 times a day).
11. Levofloxacin 500 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q24H
(every 24 hours) for 4 days.
12. Metoprolol Tartrate 25 mg Tablet Sig: 1.5 Tablets PO BID (2
times a day): hold for SBP<90.
13. Insulin Regular Human  Subcutaneous
14. Heparin Flush CVL  (100 units/ml) 1 ml IV DAILY:PRN
10ml NS followed by 1ml of 100 units/ml heparin (100 units
heparin) each lumen QD and PRN.  Inspect site every shift
15. Furosemide 20 mg IV BID
16. Metoprolol 5 mg IV Q4H:PRN
administer for Afib control to keep HR below 100.
17. Insulin NPH Human Recomb 300 unit/3 mL Insulin Pen Sig: One
(1) 10 untis Subcutaneous twice a day: Please monitor FSBS
closely and adjust dose if needed; Have RISS in place for
corrections until NPH dose is adequate.
18. Protonix 40 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO twice a day.


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Discharge Diagnosis:
1. stroke with hemorrhagic conversion
2. s/p craniotomy
3. diabetes
4. hypertension
5. paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
6. bacteremia
7. pneumonia

Discharge Condition:
Fair; Dense L-hemiplegia; eye opening apraxia; non-verbal.


Discharge Instructions:
-trach: continue care; try to wean
-PEG: continue care
-PICC line placed 06-04: fine for use; care per protocol
-s/p R-craniotomy: do not touch/press her R-head
-note that skull is stored in her abdomen
-Please provide PT, prevent contractures in her neck and L
extremities.
-FSBS, continue ISS, FSBS; adjust NPH further
-continue seizure prophylaxis

Followup Instructions:
Please check dilantin levels weekly. Corrected level, based on
albumin, 07-20.
.
Please follow up with neurosurgery: Office appointment 08-27 at 10.30. Please call d9525c9b-d750-96dd-7711-01822b3c55d8 to confirm the appointment,
to get further instructions and information, or to re-schedule
the appointment if needed.
.
Please follow up at the Effingham Hospital Clinic with Dr. Wilkerson/Dr.
Wilkerson in 3 months. Please call 2aa55e21-bc89-de67-89d0-3bf8b6555294 to schedule an
appointment and to receive instructions.
.
Please schedule a MRI-MRA of the head for follow up. This can be
done when she is seen at the Norton Audubon Hospital Clinic.


                             Nicholas Colon Crystal Hancock MD, 45735

Completed by:2007-10-04
